description: Adventure Maps, Multi-Digimon Party, Jogress and Full Roster Wiring - sixteen map backgrounds become places that accrue your steps, supply your battle opponents and hold the Digitama you can find; the save grows from one Digimon to a box of many with exactly one active and the rest frozen; two owned Digimon fuse by Jogress and hand one of their eggs back; the light button leaves the Digimon's play area; and the 780 playable Digimon that today have no evolution edge at all get wired into the graph. Source PRD: tasks/prd-adventure-maps-party-and-jogress.md

userStories.notes: Light button is now ToolbarItem(.topBarLeading), mirroring the Dex book; LightLayer draws the scrim only and LightButtonLayout.inset is gone. Build green, 1324 tests, 0 failures. Measured on 41mm and 46mm, pre- vs post-story builds: the lamp's old 24x24pt home in the slot's top-leading corner is now EMPTY (0 lit px over 150 frames, both screens); the new glyph clears the slot's top edge by 64px/69px, so no button can overlap the sprite at ANY walk offset; the sprite's box and the Dex button are pixel-identical before and after; US-112 is intact (sprite peak 255->38 = x0.15, chrome identical on and off); the toolbar lamp stays at peak 255 with the light out. userStories.notes: MapBackgroundView draws the selected map at 0.35 inside a backgroundPreferenceValue off SpriteSlotBoundsKey - the same anchor the scrim uses - so it is behind the sprite and below the scrim by construction. Build green, 1331 tests (1324 + 7 new), 0 failures. On matched walk frames all 2916 of the sprite's pixels are BYTE-IDENTICAL with a map behind them, on all three maps: the map is strictly behind the Digimon. Scrim: the map band's mean luma 63.485 -> 31.721 at semi (x0.4997) -> 9.480 at off (x0.1493), exactly dimOpacity, while chrome above and below is identical to three decimals with the light on and out. unlockedBy forms a single linear chain: map 1 has none, every other map names its predecessor
Test: MapCatalog.bundled decodes and holds exactly 16 maps
Test: every assetName resolves to a non-nil image at runtime
Tests pass

userStories.notes: Resources/maps.json (16 maps) + Sources/MapCatalog.swift (AdventureMap, DigitamaSlot, MapCatalog) in the Roster.bundled shape - hand-written decoder, fatalError on undecodable data. Build green, 1348 tests (1331 + 17 new), 0 failures. BUNDLING: no project.yml change was needed and none was made; verified the file is really in the built app - DigiVPet.app/maps.json, 28644 bytes - and every test reads it through MapCatalog.load() off Bundle.main. ASSETS: all 16 assetNames resolve to a non-nil UIImage at runtime (test), and ls confirmed the 16 imagesets in Resources/Assets.xcassets. FR-6: the test spells the table out verbatim rather than deriving it from the file - order, tier and totalSteps all match, plus monotonic totals and non-decreasing tiers. CHAIN: map 1 has no unlockedBy, every other names its predecessor, exactly one starting map, and walking the chain from the start reaches all 16. SCOPE NOTE, deliberate: opponentPool and digitamaSlots are AUTHORED here, not left empty. No later story authors them and US-121/122/128 all consume them, so empty lists would have made those stories untestable. 170 opponent ids across the 16 pools and all 57 Digitama were resolved from DISPLAY NAMES against roster.json by a throwaway script (/tmp/us116/build_maps.py, not committed) that hard-errors on an unknown name, an ambiguous name, or a dexOnly entry - so no id here was typed by hand or invented. Caught by it: 'Rapidmon' is two roster entries (rapidmon Perfect, rapidmon_armor Armor-Hybrid) and had to be pinned by id. Digitama follow FR-12's suggested assignment exactly; a test asserts all 57 are placed and none twice. Conditions scale with tier (FR-13) and every one has a non-empty hint and a known ConditionMetric, asserted by a test. ONE JUDGEMENT CALL: FR-13 names health.handwashing for 06_industrial/Pulse, which is in the family that is typically empty on real hardware - authored as FR-13 asks but PAIRED with health.steps so it is never the sole gate, per US-128's rule. Every other slot avoids that family entirely. US-117 now has real data to validate.

userStories.title: Map catalog validator
userStories.description: As a developer, I need bad map data to fail loudly in a test rather than quietly at runtime, the way EvolutionGraphValidator already does for the evolution graph. Full reasoning: tasks/prd-adventure-maps-party-and-jogress.md, US-117.
acceptanceCriteria: New Sources/MapCatalogValidator.swift, in the same shape as EvolutionGraphValidator (returns findings, does not throw)
Rejects an assetName with no matching imageset
Rejects an opponentPool id absent from the roster
Rejects an opponentPool id whose roster entry is dexOnly - a dexOnly Digimon has no animated sheet and can never be an opponent
Rejects a digitamaSlots id that is not a roster entry at Stage.digitama
Rejects an unlockedBy that names no map, and rejects any cycle in the unlock chain
Rejects a DigitamaSlot condition with a blank hint
Test: the validator reports ZERO findings over the shipped maps.json
Test: each rejection rule fires on a hand-built bad fixture
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Sources/MapCatalogValidator.swift in the EvolutionGraphValidator shape: MapValidationError (8 cases) + MapCatalog.validate(roster:assetExists:) returning findings in map order with the unlock cycles last, never throwing, plus MapCatalog.assetExists() as the injectable real check. Build green, 1363 tests (1348 + 15 new), 0 failures. EVERY REJECTION RULE FIRES ON A FIXTURE, each one the sound two-map catalog with exactly one thing broken so the reported error can only be the break: missingAsset (unknown imageset, and separately an empty assetName through the REAL check), unknownOpponent, dexOnlyOpponent, unknownDigitama, notADigitama (a Child in a slot), unknownUnlockedBy, unlockCycle (a 3-map loop, a map that unlocks itself, and a loop three maps hang off reported ONCE), emptyConditionHint (whitespace-only). A control test asserts the sound catalog reports []. AC8 run against the REAL roster and the REAL asset catalog rather than stubs - MapCatalog.load().validate() reports zero findings over the shipped 16 maps, 170 opponent ids and 57 Digitama slots - and a companion test proves the real assetExists is a check at all ('01_grassland' true, '17_moonbase' false, '' false), so the zero cannot be a stub saying yes to everything. Ordering choice worth knowing: a slot's blank-hint findings are collected BEFORE its roster lookup, so a broken slot id does not hide them and the author fixes both in one run - the same reason EvolutionGraphValidator checks conditions before the edge target. Cycle detection walks up from EVERY map, not just the roots: a cycle nothing hangs off is invisible to a walk from the starting map, which is exactly what the US-116 reachability test cannot see.

userStories.title: Steps accrue to the selected map
userStories.description: As a player, I want the steps I walk to count toward the map I chose, so a map is a place I make progress in rather than a backdrop. Full reasoning: tasks/prd-adventure-maps-party-and-jogress.md, US-118.
acceptanceCriteria: Per-map recorded steps and a finishedAt date persist across launches
ONLY steps read while that map is selected accrue to it; steps banked under a different map never move

Accrual is credited from the SAME already-deduplicated step deltas the energy path uses (HealthMetricReader / MetricLedger), so walking 1000 steps credits the map 1000 and not 2000 when two reads overlap
Test: two overlapping fixture reads credit the map once
Test: switching maps mid-day leaves already-credited steps where they were
A map is marked finished the first time recorded >= totalSteps, with a timestamp; crossing the total again does not re-fire
Recorded steps are NOT capped at the total - the counter keeps climbing past a finish
The clock and the health reader are injected; no test waits real time and no test queries live HealthKit
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Sources/MapProgress.swift: a single global @Model MapProgress (selectedMapId + [mapId: steps] + [mapId: finishedAt]) in the MetricLedger dictionary shape, plus MapStepCreditor beside EnergyCreditor/MetricCreditor. GameStore gains MapProgress to the schema and loadOrCreateMapProgress(); MainScreenModel gains maps:, mapProgress, selectMap(_:) and creditMapSteps. Build green, 1386 tests (1363 + 23 new), 0 failures, every new test also observed passing on its own. DE-DUPLICATION IS SHARED, NOT COPIED (AC3): the delta comes from a new MetricLedger.claim(_:dayTotal:now:calendar:) which is now the ONE place the day-roll and the max(0, total - baseline) rule live - MetricCreditor was refactored onto it, so the map spends the same delta any later metric consumer will, rather than keeping a second baseline that can drift. The step total reaches it through a new HealthEnergySource.dayReadings, which keeps ONE read in both shapes: byEnergyType for energy, quantities[.steps] for the map. Reading HealthKit a second time would have been two answers about one day. AC4 (1000 steps read twice credits 1000) and the growth case (1000 then 1600 credits 1600) both pass through the real refresh. AC2/AC5: a map is credited only what is read WHILE it is selected - steps banked before any map was chosen are dropped rather than parked, and switching mid-day leaves 600 on the first map and puts only the later 400 on the second. Deliberate: with no map selected the delta is claimed and DROPPED, because not claiming it would let a selection made at 18:00 retroactively bank the whole day. FINISH (AC6/AC7): stamped the first time recorded >= totalSteps, boundary pinned in both directions (999 no, 1000 yes), a second and third crossing keep the FIRST stamp, and the counter runs to 4500 past a 1000-step map. Clock and reader injected throughout; no test waits real time or touches live HealthKit. Progress outlives the Digimon - a test rebirths through GameStore and the 1000 steps and the selection are still there. NOT IN SCOPE BUT FOUND, see progress.txt: MetricCreditor has NEVER been wired into refresh (US-058 deferred it to a story that was never written), so stage/lifetime metric totals are still zero in the shipped app and the 78 window:stage health conditions in evolutions.json cannot fire. Untouched here on purpose - wiring it changes evolution eligibility across the whole game and is its own story. US-123 folds these three fields onto PlayerProfile; the shape was chosen to make that a move, not a rewrite.

userStories.title: Map list screen
userStories.description: As a player, I want to see every map, my progress on it, and which ones are still locked. Full reasoning: tasks/prd-adventure-maps-party-and-jogress.md, US-119.
acceptanceCriteria: New MapListView, pushed onto the existing NavigationStack so it keeps a back button
One row per map in tier order showing name, the map asset as a small thumbnail, and progress
Progress renders exactly as '1222 / 25000' - space, slash, space, no abbreviation and no rounding
A finished map shows a finished mark; the selected map shows a selected mark; the two marks are visually different from each other
A locked map shows a lock, hides its Digitama and opponent pool, and states its unlock condition in one line: 'Finish <previous map name>'
Tapping a locked map does NOT change the selection
Verify on the watchOS Simulator: screenshot MapListView on 41mm and 46mm with map 16 finished, so the progress text is at its widest (5-digit / 5-digit) - nothing truncates
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Sources/MapListView.swift: MapListRow (a pure value built from the catalog + MapProgress), MapListMarks, MapListSelector and MapListView, pushed onto ContentView's existing NavigationStack beside the Dex so it keeps its back button. MainScreenModel gains mapRows. Build green, 1416 tests (1386 + 30 new), 0 failures, all 30 also observed passing on their own. AC3 spelled exactly: progressText is string interpolation, NOT a NumberFormatter - a formatter is locale-dependent and would render '25,000' in en_US and '25.000' in de_DE for a criterion that names one exact string; tests pin '1222 / 25000' and assert no comma, no dot, no non-breaking space. AC4: the finished mark (checkmark.seal.fill, green) and the selected mark (figure.walk.circle.fill, orange) differ in BOTH glyph and tint, asserted pairwise, and a row can carry both at once - which is why one channel could not do the job. isFinished reads the STAMP, not recorded >= total, so a map retuned longer in a later build stays finished. AC5: locked = predecessor not finished; a locked row's contents is nil (Digitama and opponent pool hidden), its unlockLine is 'Finish <previous displayName>', and over the shipped chain all 15 locked rows name a real map by display name. AC6: MapListSelector.selection(tapping:current:) hands `current` straight back for a locked row - not nil, since 'no change' and 'no map' are different answers - and the view also disables the button. SCREENSHOTS on 41mm (Series 9, watchOS 10.4) and 46mm (Series 11, watchOS 26.4), four states each: map 16 finished reads '50000 / 50000' IN FULL on both, no truncation and no ellipsis, plus a mixed state (both marks) and a locked state (lock glyph, dimmed row, 'Finish City Dusk', no eggs/foes line). THE SCREENSHOT CAUGHT A REAL BUG, see progress.txt: the -mapListDemo seed was not idempotent, so a second launch double-credited and map 16 read '100000 / 50000'; the seed now clears first. Incidental proof from that run: even a 6-digit/5-digit figure does not truncate on 41mm. New DEBUG flags -mapListDemo / -mapListPartialDemo / -mapListBottomDemo / -mapProgressResetDemo, the last being the inverse the Codebase Patterns rule demands since the first two write to the save; both simulator containers were left reset and photographed clean. US-120's strip pushes MapListView(rows: model.mapRows) { model.selectMap($0) } - the seam is already what that story wants.

Test: the same over 30 days, so the offset is not quietly clamped to a day
BackgroundRefresh and the HealthKit observers process the active Digimon only
The complication snapshot publishes the active Digimon
No notification is ever sent about a frozen Digimon

userStories.title: Jogress data file, model and validator

Test: each rejection rule fires on a hand-built bad fixture

userStories.title: Author the Jogress recipes
userStories.description: As a player, I want the real Jogress fusions from the Color devices. Source them from Resources/Digimon_Color_And_Pendulum_Color_Evolution_Trees.md first. WARNING recorded during PRD research: a WebFetch of https://humulos.com/digimon/penc returned self-referential nonsense ('Cthyllamon + Houou = Cthyllamon', 'Mitamamon + Plesiomon = Mitamamon'). Do NOT trust a summarised fetch of that page. Full reasoning: tasks/prd-adventure-maps-party-and-jogress.md, US-131.
acceptanceCriteria: Every participant is verified present on disk with ls, and verified non-dexOnly, BEFORE being written

The four pairs the local document states verbatim are present: BlitzGreymon + CresGarurumon -> Omegamon Alter-S; Darkdramon + BanchoLeomon -> Chaosmon; Mugendramon + Darkdramon -> Chaosdramon; WarGreymon + MetalGarurumon -> Omegamon
Additional Pendulum Color recipes are added ONLY where the pairing is confirmed against a named source. An unconfirmed pairing is LEFT OUT and named in notes - a guessed recipe fails this story
The source used for each added recipe is recorded in notes
Known-unusable recipes are listed in notes rather than silently dropped. Already known: Stingmon is dexOnly, so XV-mon + Stingmon -> Paildramon cannot ship
The US-130 validator reports zero findings over the authored file
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Perform a Jogress
userStories.description: As a player, I want to fuse two of my Digimon into something stronger and get one of their eggs back. Full reasoning: tasks/prd-adventure-maps-party-and-jogress.md, US-132.
acceptanceCriteria: A Jogress entry point in PartyView offers only pairs the player actually OWNS, both alive, that match a recipe and whose conditions are all met
When no such pair exists the entry point states why in one line rather than disappearing
Performing a Jogress removes both parents from the box, creates the result as a new owned Digimon, and makes it active
Both parents' origin Digitama ids stop being held (US-127), and ONE of the two, chosen at random with an injected generator, is granted back to the box immediately as an unhatched Digitama
The result is recorded in the Dex
The whole thing is ONE saved transaction - a failure part-way leaves the box exactly as it was, with both parents alive and intact
It plays the existing EvolutionCeremonyView rather than a new animation
The US-129 failsafe check runs afterwards
Test: an eligible pair fuses; the returned egg is one of the two parents' and is deterministic under a seeded generator; an ineligible pair is refused; a mid-transaction failure rolls back
Verify on the watchOS Simulator: screenshot the Jogress entry point in PartyView and the ceremony it plays
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

The tree is reachable from a Digitama, so the whole line is playable end to end

notes records how many orphans this story removed, counted with the script in Appendix B of the PRD
